title: Warm up
content: This slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrations of drivers inside cars viewing distinct cityscapes. Each city is depicted with unique visual elements representing its character, such as traffic and landmarks in Beijing, mountain architecture in Chongqing, and technology integration in Hangzhou.
key_claims: Beijing is shown with urban traffic and historical landmarks like the Forbidden City.
Chongqing is depicted with mountainous terrain and traditional architecture.
Hangzhou is shown with a driver using smart technology and a lake landscape.

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This title slide introduces the presentation's theme, 'A Ride Through China,' using imagery of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture. It sets the stage for exploring China's development through the lens of different cities.
key_claims: The presentation theme is 'A Ride Through China.'
Visuals include a taxi sign, the Forbidden City, and traditional pagodas representing different regions.

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure of Session 1, which involves investigating and researching city development. It presents a three-step process: identifying Local Features, applying Development Strategies, and achieving Outcomes & Positive Impact, illustrated alongside a stylized city map.
key_claims: The session follows a three-step research framework.
Step 3 is achieving Outcomes & Positive Impact.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This interactive slide presents a matching exercise linking three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—to their respective development strategies. Lines connect each city to a strategy: technology & tradition integration, sustainable urban development, or tourism & economic growth.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with 'Sustainable urban development.'
Chongqing is matched with 'Tourism & Economic growth.'
Hangzhou is matched with 'Technology & tradition integration (融合).'

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide reveals the correct pairing of each city with its development strategy through the perspective of taxi drivers, using the same in-car images from the warm-up. It concludes with the key theme that China is an evolving nation where each city tailors strategies to its local context, reflecting the principle of '因地制宜' (adapting to local conditions).
key_claims: China is an evolving nation with unique development strategies per city.
Each city develops strategies based on local features (因地制宜) to promote development.
